title_clean: We're Divorcing Like It's 1959: The Divorce Rate Is Falling, But So Is the Rate of Americans Saying 'I Do.'
blurb: An analysis showing the falling US divorce rate isn't a sign of healthier marriages — it's a sign that fewer Americans are marrying in the first place.
A newsletter roundup on how Gen Z's relationship to school is shifting, from K-12 disengagement to rising skepticism about the value of a four-year degree.
ambiguous_note: Title alone is generic; content inferred from the URL slug referencing higher ed and K-12.

blurb: A Movember research report mapping the media ecosystem young men move through — the platforms, creators, and content shaping their ideas about masculinity, mental health, and identity.

title_clean: Is Partying Dead, or Are You Just Old?
blurb: A New York Times feature questioning the narrative that Gen Z has abandoned nightlife, arguing that partying hasn't disappeared so much as changed shape.
A Notion-based research hub compiling secondary research on Gen Z connection, digital life, and mental health as part of a project referred to as DCDX.
ambiguous_note: DCDX appears to be a youth-research initiative (hosted at genzdesigns.notion.site), but the sponsoring org and full scope of the project aren't confirmable from the title alone.

blurb: A companion Notion hub to the same DCDX research project, gathering social-listening findings and content examples that illustrate Gen Z digital culture.
ambiguous_note: Title indicates a social-listening/content archive linked to the DCDX project, but specifics aren't confirmable from the title alone.

blurb: Management professor Suzy Welch argues that Gen Z and millennial burnout has less to do with working harder than prior generations and more to do with a lost sense of hope about the future.
A data-driven look at whether young people today are genuinely worse off economically than previous generations, weighing housing costs, wages, and wealth trends against the popular narrative.
Rather than adding another hot take, a journalist asks young people directly how AI, the job market, and housing costs shape their sense of whether their generation is 'screwed.'
Harvard's Institute of Politics presents its latest national poll tracking young Americans' political attitudes, institutional trust, and outlook on the country's direction.
A Gallup analysis linking rising loneliness among young Americans to growing tolerance for political violence.
A TikTok from creator Tinx weighing a lower-paying remote job against a higher-paying in-office one, capturing a trade-off many young workers are currently debating.
ambiguous_note: Title only gives two dollar figures with no framing; the video's actual argument is inferred rather than confirmed.

blurb: GoFundMe's 2024 report on how giving behavior is changing, including how younger generations are showing up for peer-to-peer and community causes online.
ambiguous_note: Report's specific findings are inferred from its title and publisher; the source page is a campaign landing page rather than the report itself.

blurb: An Atlantic essay on the writer's growing alarm at rising antisemitism among young Americans and what's fueling it.
A New York Times focus group in which eleven Gen Z voters speak candidly about the military, America's role in the world, and their sense of being let down by older generations.
An interactive tool for choosing among co-creation and participatory-design methods based on a project's goals and constraints.
ambiguous_note: Tool's exact method set and design criteria are inferred from its title alone, without exploring the interactive app.
